name: Roasty-Toasty Potatoes, Carrots, and Onions
recipeIngredient: 2 Pounds mature red potatoes (about 8 small but not tiny potatoes)
1 Pound carrots (about 6 medium), washed and scrubbed, unpeeled, stem end left on
2 Large onions, quartered, peel left on
1 Tablespoon mild vegetable oil (such as corn, peanut, or canola oil)
1 Tablespoon Tamari or Shoyu soy sauce, plus additional to taste (1-2)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Bring two large pots of water to a boil.
3. Oil or spray a baking dish, or dishes, with the vegetable oil large enough to accommodate all vegetables without them touching each other.
4. Blanch potatoes in one pot of boiling water for about 6-8 minutes, then drain well and cool with cold water.
5. Blanch carrots in the second pot of boiling water for 4-6 minutes (long, skinny) or 5-6 minutes (fatter), then drain well.
6. Place potatoes, carrots, and onion quarters (with skin) in the prepared baking dish.
7. Toss vegetables with oil, rubbing it into the vegetables with hands.
8. Bake about half an hour, then drizzle with Tamari or Shoyu.
9. Using an implement, toss again to coat evenly.
10. Continue baking until potatoes and carrots are soft (can be pierced easily with a fork) and browned on edges; this takes about 45-55 minutes total.
11. Serve hot immediately, but these vegetables also do well staying warm for up to 30 minutes or even an hour before serving.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings
